strengths: Clearly identifies three specific real-world objects and situates each in its appropriate context.
Provides coherent links between each object and the IA prompt, supported by evidence such as dictionary definitions and demographic statistics.
Engages with relevant TOK concepts (e.g., personal vs shared knowledge, interpretations) to frame the discussion.
weaknesses: Explanations often drift into descriptive or background information without sufficiently analysing how each object exemplifies community-specific knowledge.
Key TOK concepts are introduced without precise definitions or consistently applied to the objects.
Citations and reference formatting are uneven and some in-text references are missing or incomplete.
Several passages contain unfinished sentences and repetition, which undermines clarity and coherence.
There is no concluding synthesis that brings together insights from all three objects under the overarching TOK prompt.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exhibition meets the requirements for band 5â6: it clearly identifies three objects in real-world contexts, explains links to the prompt, and supports many points with evidence. However, the treatment of TOK concepts lacks precision and depth in places, the analysis sometimes reverts to descriptive background, and structural and citation weaknesses (e.g., unfinished sentences, inconsistent referencing, absence of a concluding synthesis) prevent it from achieving the higher level of sustained clarity, focus and rigorous justification required for band 7â8. Assigning a score of 6 reflects the solid fulfilment of the criteria alongside these notable limitations.

annotations.comment: The student effectively identifies the La EspaÃ±ola olive can and situates it within the Spanish âappetitivoâ tradition, providing a clear real-world context for the second object.

The paragraph offers interesting cultural background on appetizers but drifts from analysing how olive-related knowledge is uniquely held by specific communities. A sharper focus on the prompt is needed.
The term âculturally relativeâ is introduced without definition or deeper exploration; linking this directly to TOK concepts (e.g. cultural bias, relativism) would enhance the justification.
The student clearly identifies the Pandora charm bracelet as the first object and situates it within personal memorabilia, fulfilling the requirement to present a specific, real-world object.
The in-text reference to the Theory of Knowledge source lacks precise citation details. Providing proper page or section references would strengthen the credibility of the argument.
There is no clear definition or discussion of âpersonalâ versus âsharedâ knowledge in TOK terms; explicitly defining these concepts would help link the object more directly to the prompt.
No external evidence is provided to support the claim that olive knowledge belongs solely to Spaniards; adding statistics or cultural studies would bolster the argument.
The student cites the Cambridge Dictionary and the Theory of Knowledge text but does not integrate these definitions critically with the object, missing an opportunity to deepen the TOK analysis.
The use of demographic statistics (85.3% Romanian Orthodox, 4% global population) provides concrete evidence to support the communal nature of Orthodox knowledge.
The student stops short of explaining what these percentages imply about knowledge ownership; explicitly linking these figures to the boundaries of shared knowledge would deepen the analysis.
The reference list is comprehensive but citation formats vary (italics, punctuation); adopting a consistent style (e.g., APA or MLA) will enhance academic rigor.
Some sources cited in the text (e.g. Theory of Knowledge textbook) do not appear in the bibliography; ensure all in-text references are matched in the reference list.
The student repeats the general definition of knowledge without adding new insight; synthesising source material more succinctly will reduce redundancy and sharpen analysis.

The discussion remains largely descriptive, focusing on how the bracelet is used rather than analysing how it exemplifies community-specific knowledge boundaries in the TOK prompt. More critical reflection is needed.

The justification ends in mid-sentence (âOverall it can be said that the knowledge surrounding the cultureâ¦â), which undermines clarity. The student should complete and refine this concluding statement.
The student clearly introduces the Romanian Orthodox prayer book, linking it to familial transmission of religious knowledge, which effectively grounds the third object in a real-world context.
The narrative about the studentâs father âdoes his best toâ¦â is cut off, indicating an incomplete argument. The student should finish this thought and tie it to the TOK prompt.
Claims about Orthodox practices rely solely on personal anecdote; referencing a scholarly or theological source would provide stronger support for the analysis.
The anecdote about family customs offers personal context but stops short of analysing how shared religious knowledge differs between insiders and outsiders in the Orthodox community.

There is no explicit use of TOK vocabulary (e.g. âcommunity knowledgeâ, âinterpretationâ) to frame how the prayer book embodies community-specific knowledge, weakening the theoretical linkage.
The exhibition lacks a concluding paragraph synthesising insights from all three objects. A concise conclusion would reinforce the overarching argument on community-specific knowledge.
The bibliography entries are not in alphabetical order; organising them alphabetically will improve navigability and professional presentation.
The image description is accurate but unused in the analysis; integrating visual details (e.g. label text, design cues) into the TOK discussion would strengthen evidence.
Transitions between objects are abrupt; adding brief signposting sentences would guide the reader and clarify the exhibitionâs structure.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

The exhibition presents
three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of
types of object rather
than of specific
real-world objects. Links
between the objects
and the selected IA
prompt are made, but
these are minimal,
tenuous, or it is not
clear what the student is
trying to convey.
There is very little
justification offered for
the inclusion of each
object in the exhibition.
The commentary on the
objects is highly
descriptive or consists
only of unsupported
assertions.

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
